original_query: select n_name, sum(l_extendedprice * (1 - l_discount)) as revenue from customer, orders, lineitem, supplier, nation, region where c_custkey = o_custkey and l_orderkey = o_orderkey and l_suppkey = s_suppkey and c_nationkey = s_nationkey and s_nationkey = n_nationkey and n_regionkey = r_regionkey and r_name = 'EUROPE' and o_orderdate >= date '1995-01-01' and o_orderdate < date '1995-01-01' + interval '1' year group by n_name order by revenue desc;
rewritten_query:
SELECT 
    n.n_name, 
    SUM(l.l_extendedprice * (1 - l.l_discount)) AS revenue
FROM 
    region r
    JOIN nation n ON r.r_regionkey = n.n_regionkey
    JOIN supplier s ON s.s_nationkey = n.n_nationkey
    JOIN customer c ON c.c_nationkey = s.s_nationkey
    JOIN orders o ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey
    JOIN lineitem l ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey AND l.l_suppkey = s.s_suppkey
WHERE 
    r.r_name = 'EUROPE'
    AND o.o_orderdate >= DATE '1995-01-01'
    AND o.o_orderdate < DATE '1996-01-01'
GROUP BY 
    n.n_name
ORDER BY 
    revenue DESC;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Converted implicit joins to explicit JOIN syntax for better readability and to help the optimizer understand the join relationships
Reordered joins to filter data early - starting with region, then nation, supplier, customer, orders, and finally lineitem to reduce the dataset size in subsequent joins
Used table aliases to make the query more readable and maintainable
